evidence_excerpts: By the end of the lesson, learners will be able to identify and name the main components of the human muscular-skeletal system (bones, muscles, and joints).

evidence_excerpts: Prior knowledge activation: Discussing why the body has a firm shape and how it moves. The learner explains the purpose of the internal body framework. The body has a firm framework.; This framework allows us to stand and move.; Without it, we would be like a heap of cloth.

evidence_excerpts: Exploration: Observing a skeleton chart to see how bones are arranged. The learner identifies the major parts of the human skeleton on a chart. The skeleton is the bony framework of the body.; It is made of many bones of different shapes.; It supports the whole body.
